Both outputs demonstrate strong technical competency with proper pytest structure, PySpark operations, and error handling. Key differences: scope breadth, ABAP-specific considerations, and test coverage depth.

Both outputs address PySpark testing but with different semantic focus. pytest_pyspark_tests concentrates on data transformation logic with status assignment and aggregation (lines 15-25), while ABAP_to_PySpark_Conversion_Tester addresses comprehensive ABAP-to-PySpark migration scenarios including file handling, DML operations, and ABAP-specific constructs (lines 5-15). Semantic alignment exists in core PySpark testing approach but diverges in business context and scope.

Both maintain consistent test naming conventions and proper PySpark session management.
